agents.description: Read-only Soridormi robot status, mode, and battery state.

tools.name: soridormi.robot.get_status
tools.description: Read Soridormi status including mode, backend, safety state, and active task metadata.

llm_hints.when_to_use: Use before planning or executing robot movement.

tools.name: soridormi.robot.get_mode
tools.description: Read the current Soridormi runtime mode such as sim, hardware_dry_run, or hardware.

tools.name: soridormi.robot.get_battery
tools.description: Read battery percentage and power safety status when available.

availability.reason: Sim may return null battery percent.

agents.description: Create and execute short velocity-command motion plans. Does not expose raw motor commands.

tools.name: soridormi.motion.create_plan
tools.description: Create a bounded short-range velocity-command plan. This does not move the robot.

llm_hints.when_to_use: Use for short movements like walking forward a little, turning, or stopping.
llm_hints.when_not_to_use: Do not use for named-location navigation; use a future nav agent instead.

tools.name: soridormi.motion.execute_plan
tools.description: Execute a previously created and validated Soridormi motion plan.

confirmation.reason: This tool can move the robot.

llm_hints.when_to_use: Use only after create_plan succeeds and Chromie has handled user confirmation.

tools.name: soridormi.motion.stop
tools.description: Stop current Soridormi motion as quickly as the safe command layer allows.

tools.name: soridormi.motion.cancel
tools.description: Cancel the current Soridormi motion plan and transition to stop.

agents.description: Discover, plan, and execute named body skills through opaque Soridormi-owned plans.

tools.name: soridormi.skill.list
tools.description: List versioned named skills and parameter schemas.

tools.name: soridormi.skill.create_plan
tools.description: Validate a named skill and create an opaque Soridormi-owned plan. Planning does not move the robot.

tools.name: soridormi.skill.execute_plan
tools.description: Execute an opaque named-skill plan. The runtime adapter may move the MuJoCo robot in sim mode; hardware shadow/dry-run profiles remain no-motion until a hardware adapter is implemented.

confirmation.reason: This exercises a body-skill provider contract.

agents.description: Contract-first embodied task API. It records structured goals and refuses missing, unsafe, or unsupported capability paths; this surface does not execute robot motion yet.

tools.name: soridormi.task.get_capabilities
tools.description: Read Soridormi-owned embodied task readiness, required subsystems, missing subsystems, and no-motion task API status.

llm_hints.when_to_use: Use before planning embodied tasks to inspect what Soridormi can dry-run, hold, redirect, or fail closed.
llm_hints.physical_execution_boundary: This reports task readiness only. It does not authorize physical motion or raw low-level control.

tools.name: soridormi.task.preview
tools.description: Preview Soridormi's no-motion interpretation of a structured embodied task goal without creating a persistent task record.

llm_hints.when_to_use: Use before task submission when Chromie needs to explain, clarify, or verify Soridormi's embodied interpretation.
llm_hints.when_not_to_use: Do not treat preview as task execution or as a persistent task id; use soridormi.task.submit to create a task record.
llm_hints.plan_step_boundary: plan_steps describe Soridormi-owned embodied planning layers and never contain raw low-level robot control.
llm_hints.next_action_boundary: recommended_next_actions are routing hints for Chromie; they are not execution receipts or low-level commands.

tools.name: soridormi.task.submit
tools.description: Submit a structured embodied task goal. The M11 contract surface validates and records the request, but returns no_motion=true until task execution is implemented.

llm_hints.when_to_use: Use for structured embodied goals that should remain Soridormi-owned instead of becoming velocity recipes.
llm_hints.when_not_to_use: Do not use for immediate stop/emergency behavior; call soridormi.motion.stop, soridormi.motion.cancel, or soridormi.safety.emergency_stop.
llm_hints.plan_step_boundary: plan_steps describe Soridormi-owned embodied planning layers and never contain raw low-level robot control.
llm_hints.next_action_boundary: recommended_next_actions are routing hints for Chromie; they are not execution receipts or low-level commands.

tools.name: soridormi.task.status
tools.description: Read a previously submitted Soridormi embodied task status.

tools.name: soridormi.task.events
tools.description: Read structured lifecycle events for a Soridormi embodied task.

tools.name: soridormi.task.cancel
tools.description: Cancel a non-terminal Soridormi embodied task request.

agents.description: Soridormi safety validation, motion monitoring, and emergency stop capabilities.

tools.name: soridormi.safety.monitor_motion
tools.description: Monitor safety state while a Soridormi physical-motion node is running.

tools.name: soridormi.safety.emergency_stop
tools.description: Hard-stop Soridormi and abort robot motion. This safety tool can preempt other tasks.

llm_hints.when_to_use: Use immediately for fall, collision risk, emergency stop, or user stop/cancel.

agents.description: Test-only provider fault injection; never model visible.

tools.name: soridormi.testing.configure_fault
tools.description: Configure one deterministic provider fault.

tools.name: soridormi.testing.clear_faults
tools.description: Clear all configured provider faults.

rules: Soridormi tools do not implement Chromie speech, TTS, ASR, or user confirmation.
Chromie must use chromie.ask_confirmation before physical-motion execution unless the action is stop/cancel/emergency_stop.
Chromie must cover soridormi.motion.execute_plan with soridormi.safety.monitor_motion.
Chromie must use soridormi.motion.create_plan before soridormi.motion.execute_plan.
Chromie must cover soridormi.skill.execute_plan with soridormi.safety.monitor_motion.
Chromie must use soridormi.skill.create_plan before soridormi.skill.execute_plan.
Chromie may use soridormi.task.get_capabilities to inspect Soridormi-owned embodied readiness before preview or submission.
Chromie may use soridormi.task.preview before user confirmation or task submission to inspect Soridormi's no-motion embodied interpretation.
Chromie may submit structured embodied goals through soridormi.task.submit, but M11 task submission is contract-only and no-motion until Soridormi adds task execution.
Chromie must use soridormi.task.status or soridormi.task.events to monitor task-level requests.
Do not expose raw motor, joint, or torque controls to LLM-generated graphs.
stop and emergency_stop may preempt any running motion task.
